context.projectOverview: Sprint 3 of cockpit-integration. Adds an event-stream MCP tool that lets a cockpit client subscribe to live progress events for a run — tails .bober/history.jsonl + .bober/telemetry/<date>.jsonl filtered by runId, streams them via MCP server-to-client notifications. Must handle backpressure, reconnection, and clean shutdown.

completedSprints.title: Multi-run RunManager + persistence
completedSprints.summary: Map<runId,RunState>, atomic state.json, load() reconciliation, back-compat shims. 1142 tests.

completedSprints.title: Multi-run MCP tools
completedSprints.summary: bober_list_active_runs, bober_get_run_status, bober_abort_run. RunState widened with 'aborted'. 1169 tests.

context.criticalContext: MCP protocol notifications: the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk Server class supports server.sendLoggingMessage() and the newer notifications/message capability for streaming events. The implementation choice (resourceUpdated, sampling notifications, or a custom notification name) should be made by the generator based on what the SDK supports — read node_modules/@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/dist/cjs/types.js or similar to find the right idiom. For event sources, .bober/history.jsonl + .bober/telemetry/<date>.jsonl are append-only JSONL files; tail them with fs.watch or a poll loop. Filter events for the given runId (events include runId field when applicable).
